title: Do not test CNI in CI
body:
WIP.

5.0 build tags out CNI, let's drop those tests from our CI. 


 Probably needs many pushes to get it green, but I'll whack at this PR until then.

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Root cause: f38 VM images do not include netavark.

Solution: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/325 in progress. Will take a few hours. I will report back.
`c20240129t175101z-f39f38d13` is ready but you can't just swap them in without the rest of #21366
Thanks Ed! Not a huge deal, this can wait for that.
Update: this PR is unlikely to merge this week. I'm sorry. Please feel free to [follow along](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/325)
Thanks @edsantiago for the new VMs, lets see how this goes :)
Looks like everything except upgrade is passing, upgrade seems to be because v4.0.0 isn't in quay. Setting that it to v4.1.0 and re-running and seeing how that goes.
How frustrating. `v4.0.0`  doesn't exist, but `v4.1.0` does. I see that you tested it. What was the failure with `v4.1.0`?

@edsantiago Looks like it the [network tests](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5362647089020928/html/upgrade_test-podman-fedora-39-root-host-boltdb.log.html) aren't passing, which I kinda expected. If you have any hunches off the top of your head, let me know, otherwise I'll dig a little deeper.
Just disable the upgrade tests. I have a TODO item to revamp those, I will make it a priority on Monday.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *ashley-cui*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,ashley-cui] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM. I'm working on upgrade tests, the fixes for boltdb/sqlite are much harder than I expected. I intend to submit my PR on or by Wednesday.
